YouTube](http://img.shields.io/badge/youtube-nf--core-FF0000?labelColor=000000&logo=youtube)](https://www.youtube.com/c/nf-core) ## Introduction **nf-core/templatetres** processes pooled TrES DNA and RNA libraries using an nf-core workflow layout. It performs raw read QC, assay-specific barcode tagging, trimming, map-driven split of pooled reads into biological groups, barcode-aware DNA alignment with duplicate marking, and RNA `FASTQ -> uSAM -> STARsolo` quantification. Default workflow sections: 1. Raw read QC with [`FastQC`](https://www.bioinformatics.babraham.ac.uk/projects/fastqc/) 2. DNA/RNA barcode tagging with vendored TrES Codon modules 3. Adapter/quality trimming with [`Trim Galore`](https://www.bioinformatics.babraham.ac.uk/projects/trim_galore/) 4. SB/MO map-driven read splitting into TrES groups and marks 5. DNA alignment, barcode filtering, duplicate marking, and bigWig generation 6. RNA uSAM generation, STARsolo alignment, filtered BAM, matrices, and bigWigs 7. Aggregated QC with [`MultiQC`](http://multiqc.info/) ## Usage > [!NOTE] > If you are new to Nextflow and nf-core, please refer to [this page](https://nf-co.re/docs/usage/installation) on how to set-up Nextflow. Make sure to [test your setup](https://nf-co.re/docs/usage/introduction#how-to-run-a-pipeline) with `-profile test` before running the workflow on actual data. First, prepare a raw-library samplesheet like this: ```csv sample,library_id,organism,modality,sb_panel,injected_base,fastq_r1,fastq_r2,fastq_i1,fastq_i2 260109_TreSeq_251121_Bcell_Tonsil_day15_DNA_S3,260109_TreSeq_251121_Bcell_Tonsil_day15_DNA_S3,human,dna,sample12,A,assets/testdata/260109_TreSeq_251121_Bcell_Tonsil_day15_DNA_S3_R1_001.fastq.gz,assets/testdata/260109_TreSeq_251121_Bcell_Tonsil_day15_DNA_S3_R2_001.fastq.gz,assets/testdata/260109_TreSeq_251121_Bcell_Tonsil_day15_DNA_S3_I1_001.fastq.gz,assets/testdata/260109_TreSeq_251121_Bcell_Tonsil_day15_DNA_S3_I2_001.fastq.gz 260109_TreSeq_251121_Bcell_Tonsil_day15_RNA_S4,260109_TreSeq_251121_Bcell_Tonsil_day15_RNA_S4,human,rna,sample12,A,assets/testdata/260109_TreSeq_251121_Bcell_Tonsil_day15_RNA_S4_R1_001.fastq.gz,assets/testdata/260109_TreSeq_251121_Bcell_Tonsil_day15_RNA_S4_R2_001.fastq.gz,assets/testdata/260109_TreSeq_251121_Bcell_Tonsil_day15_RNA_S4_I1_001.fastq.gz, ``` Each row represents one pooled library input. Demultiplexed `Normal`, `Co2`, and DNA mark outputs are not separate rows in the samplesheet; they are derived from the SB/MO map TSVs passed with `--sb_map_dna`, `--sb_map_rna`, and `--mo_map_dna`. Now, you can run the pipeline using: ```bash nextflow run nf-core/templatetres \ -profile \ --input samplesheet.csv \ --sb_map_dna assets/examples/camille/sb_map_camille.tsv \ --sb_map_rna assets/examples/camille/sb_map_camille_RNA.tsv \ --mo_map_dna assets/examples/camille/mo_map_camille.tsv \ --sb_whitelist_sample12 assets/examples/camille/sample12_whitelist.txt \ --sb_whitelist_sample8 /path/to/sample8_whitelist.txt \ --ligation_whitelist /path/to/ligation_whitelist.txt \ --dna_modality_whitelist /path/to/dna_modality_whitelist.txt \ --ref_base /path/to/reference_base \ --bwa_human /path/to/human_bwa_reference.fa \ --bwa_mouse /path/to/mouse_bwa_reference.fa \ --blacklist_human /path/to/human.blacklist.bed \ --blacklist_mouse /path/to/mouse.blacklist.bed \ --outdir ``` > [!WARNING] > Please provide pipeline parameters via the CLI or Nextflow `-params-file` option. Custom config files including those provided by the `-c` Nextflow option can be used to provide any configuration _**except for parameters**_; see [docs](https://nf-co.re/docs/usage/getting_started/configuration#custom-configuration-files). The repository-local example metadata lives under [`assets/examples/camille`](assets/examples/camille), and a stub-safe example samplesheet is provided at [`assets/samplesheet.csv`](assets/samplesheet.csv). ## Pipeline output To see the results of an example test run with a full size dataset refer to the [results](https://nf-co.re/templatetres/results) tab on the nf-core website pipeline page. For more details about the output files and reports, please refer to the [output documentation](https://nf-co.re/templatetres/output). ## Credits nf-core/templatetres was originally written by Nikita Miroshnichenko, Ahrmad Annan. We thank the TrES pipeline developers and dataset owners whose shell workflows and metadata definitions informed this nf-core implementation. ## Contributions and Support If you would like to contribute to this pipeline, please see the [contributing guidelines](.github/CONTRIBUTING.md). For further information or help, don't hesitate to get in touch on the [Slack `#templatetres` channel](https://nfcore.slack.com/channels/templatetres) (you can join with [this invite](https://nf-co.re/join/slack)). ## Citations If you use nf-core/templatetres for your analysis, please cite the pipeline release once a persistent pipeline DOI is minted for it. An extensive list of references for the tools used by the pipeline can be found in the [`CITATIONS.md`](CITATIONS.md) file. You can cite the `nf-core` publication as follows: > **The nf-core framework for community-curated bioinformatics pipelines.** > > Philip Ewels, Alexander Peltzer, Sven Fillinger, Harshil Patel, Johannes Alneberg, Andreas Wilm, Maxime Ulysse Garcia, Paolo Di Tommaso & Sven Nahnsen. > > _Nat Biotechnol._ 2020 Feb 13. doi: [10.1038/s41587-020-0439-x](https://dx.doi.org/10.1038/s41587-020-0439-x).
